title: Notholaena piloselloides
intro: Notholaena piloselloides, also known as the hairy fern or rock fern, is a species of fern native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The hairy fern has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Notholaena piloselloides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Notholaena piloselloides is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is native to the Americas and is commonly known as the "hairy fern" or "narrow-leaved fern". The classification of Notholaena piloselloides is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Pteridaceae, genus Notholaena, and species N. piloselloides. This classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other ferns. The specific classification of Notholaena piloselloides helps scientists understand its place in the plant kingdom.
uses: Notholaena piloselloides, also known as the woolly fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. In some cultures, the plant's leaves and stems are used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Notholaena piloselloides. Further studies may help to confirm its effectiveness in treating certain health conditions.
benefits: Notholaena piloselloides, also known as the hairy fern or rabbit's foot fern, is a plant native to North and South America.
